news.title: Global Market: ECB says Target 2 payment system back to normal after brief processing disruption
news.description: The European Central Bank said its Target 2 (T2) payment system resumed normal operations after a brief technical disruption delayed payment processing. The incident temporarily affected the settlement window for euro and Danish krone transactions before the ECB confirmed the issue had been resolved

news.title: Global Market: European shares steady as investors track deal activity; easyJet jumps on takeover proposal
news.description: European shares held steady as dealmaking took center stage, with a potential takeover of easyJet by Castlelake boosting travel stocks. news.title: Jefferies' Christopher Wood warns Microsoft, Meta, and Alphabet AI spending may backfire
news.description: Jefferies strategist Chris Wood has warned that Wall Street's hyperscalers risk massive capital destruction from excessive AI spending, arguing markets may soon push back against rising debt-funded investments. news.title: Global Market: Bank of England weighs leverage rule changes to boost gilt demand
news.description: Britain's government bond market could see a significant boost and borrowing costs slashed by over £1 billion annually if the Bank of England eases bank leverage rules. news.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX and the vision of off-planet industrialization
news.description: Dhirendra Kumar reflects on a compelling conversation with a friend about the future of space mining and manufacturing, suggesting potential shifts in industry that could change life on Earth. For once, he's not telling you to ignore the news.

news.title: Inside A Storied Renaissance Villa In The Hills Of Florence
news.description: Three miles from Florence, this 15th-century property comes to market—for the first time in four generations of the same prominent Italian family—for $6.5 million.

news.title: US DOJ says decision to drop Adani charges not tied to planned US investments
news.description: DOJ clarifies that dropping charges against Adani was due to legal flaws, not linked to his planned US investments.
